perturbation.original_text: 1. Definitions. : (a) The term “Confidential Information” shall include, without limitation, all information and materials, furnished by either Party to the other in connection with Auditee products and services including information transmitted in writing, orally, visually, (e.g. video terminal display) or on magnetic media, and including all proprietary information, customer & prospect lists, trade secrets, trade names or proposed trade names, methods and procedures of o peration, business or marketing plans, licensed document know -how, ideas, concepts, designs, drawings, flow charts, diagrams, quality manuals, checklists, guidelines, processes, formulae, source code materials, specifications, programs, software packages, codes and other intellectual property relating to Auditee products and services. Results of any information security audits, tests, analysis , extracts or usages carried out by the Auditor in connection with the Auditee’s products and/or services , IT infras tructure, etc. shall also be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Definitions. : (a) The term “Confidential Information” shall include, without limitation, all information and materials, furnished by either Party to the other in connection with Auditee products and services including information transmitted in writing, orally, visually, (e.g. video terminal display) or on magnetic media, and including all proprietary information, customer & prospect lists, trade secrets, trade names or proposed trade names, methods and procedures of o peration, business or marketing plans, licensed document know -how, ideas, concepts, designs, drawings, flow charts, diagrams, quality manuals, checklists, guidelines, processes, formulae, source code materials, specifications, programs, software packages, codes and other intellectual property relating to Auditee products and services. Results of any information security audits, tests, analysis , extracts or usages carried out by the Examiner in connection with the Auditee’s products and/or services , IT infras tructure, etc. shall also be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Auditor' to 'Examiner' within the definition of 'Confidential Information' creates confusion. The earlier part of the agreement identifies the auditing entity as the 'Auditor,' so introducing 'Examiner' without defining it leads to uncertainty about who is performing the audits and whose results are considered confidential. The original section of definition considers the results from the Auditor to be considered as Confidential Information, while in the changed text, the document starts to refere to 'Examiner' which could cause confusion when referring to the term 'Auditor' later in the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2 Protection of Confidential Information. With respect to any Confidential Information disclosed to it or to which it has access, Auditor affirms that it shall: (a) Use the Confidential Information as necessary only in connection with scope of audi t and in accordance with the terms and conditions contained herein; (b) Maintain the Confidential Information in strict confidence and take all reasonable steps to enforce the confidentiality obligations imposed hereunder, but in no event take less care with t he Confidential Information that the parties take to protect the confidentiality of its own proprietary and confidential information and that of its other clients; (c) Not to make or retain copy of any details of products and/or services, prototypes, busines s or marketing plans, Client lists, Proposals developed by or originating from Auditee or any of the prospective clients of Auditee. (d) Not to make or retain copy of any details of results of any information security audits, tests, analysis , extracts or usag es carried out by the Auditor in connection with the Auditee’s products and/or services, IT infrastructure, etc. without the express written consent of Auditee. (e) Not disclose or in any way assist or permit the disclosure of any Confidential Information to a ny other person or entity without the express written consent of the auditee ; and (f) Return to the auditee , or destroy, at auditee ’s discretion, any and all Confidential Information disclosed in a printed form or other permanent record, or in any other tangibl e form (including without limitation, all copies, notes, extracts, analyses, studies, summaries, records and reproductions thereof) immediately on (i) expiration or termination of this agreement , or (ii) the request of Auditee therefor. (g) Not to send Auditee ’s audit information or data and/or any such Confidential Information at any time outside India for the purpose of storage, processing , analysis or handling without the express written consent of the Auditee. (h) The auditor shall use only the best possible secure methodology to avoid confidentiality breach, while handl ing audit related data for the purpose of storage, processing, transit or analysis including sharing of information with auditee. (i) Not to engage or appoint any non -resident/foreigner to undert ake any activity related to Information Security Audit. In case of information security audits for Government/ critical sector organization, only the man power declared to CERT -In shall be deployed to carry out such audit related activities. (j) Not to discuss with any member of public, media, press, any or any other person about the nature of arrangement entered between the Auditor and the Auditee or the nature of services to be provided by Auditor to the Auditee. (k) Make sure that all the employees and/or consul tants engaged to undertake any audit on its behalf have signed the mandatory non -disclosure agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Auditor' to 'Examiner' in the definition of 'Confidential Information' introduces a misaligned terminology, leading to an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text refers specifically to 'Auditor' and their responsibilities regarding confidential information. By changing 'Auditor' to 'Examiner' in the definition, it creates uncertainty about whether the obligations outlined in the contradicted text apply to the 'Examiner' as well, creating a strong and direct conflict within the document. The definition of 'Confidential Information' now includes results from 'Examiner' which is not consistent with 'Auditor' as mentioned in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: 2 Protection of Confidential Information. With respect to any Confidential Information disclosed to it or to which it has access, Auditor affirms that it shall: (a) Use the Confidential Information as necessary only in connection with scope of audi t and in accordance with the terms and conditions contained herein; (b) Maintain the Confidential Information in strict confidence and take all reasonable steps to enforce the confidentiality obligations imposed hereunder, but in no event take less care with t he Confidential Information that the parties take to protect the confidentiality of its own proprietary and confidential information and that of its other clients;
perturbation.changed_text: 2 Protection of Confidential Information. With respect to any Confidential Information disclosed to it or to which it has access, the Inspector affirms that it shall: (a) Use the Confidential Information as necessary only in connection with scope of audi t and in accordance with the terms and conditions contained herein; (b) Maintain the Confidential Information in strict confidence and take all reasonable steps to enforce the confidentiality obligations imposed hereunder, but in no event take less care with t he Confidential Information that the parties take to protect the confidentiality of its own proprietary and confidential information and that of its other clients;
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Auditor' to 'Inspector' in Section 2 creates an internal contradiction. Throughout the document, the designated auditing party is consistently referred to as the 'Auditor.' Introducing 'Inspector' as a replacement suggests a different entity with potentially different responsibilities, even if it's not explicitly defined, thus leading to uncertainty about who is bound by the confidentiality obligations. Since there is no point in the document, where the entity is mentioned as Inspector, the confusion of its roles and responsibilities would be a point to be discussed.
perturbation.contradicted_location: 4. Permitted disclosure of audit related information :
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Permitted disclosure of audit related information : The auditor may share audit information with CERT -In or similar Government entities mandated under the law as and when called upon to do so by such agencies with prior written infor mation to the auditee.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Auditor' to 'Inspector' creates a contradiction because the contradicted text (Section 4) refers to 'the auditor'. By introducing 'Inspector' in Section 2, the document now has two potentially distinct entities responsible for auditing, leading to confusion. Since Section 4 only refers to 'the auditor,' it's unclear if the permissions granted in that section also apply to the 'Inspector,' thus creating a direct conflict regarding responsibilities and information sharing.

perturbation.original_text: (g) Not to send Auditee ’s audit information or data and/or any such Confidential Information at any time outside India for the purpose of storage, processing , analysis or handling without the express written consent of the Auditee. (h) The auditor shall use only the best possible secure methodology to avoid confidentiality breach, while handl ing audit related data for the purpose of storage, processing, transit or analysis including sharing of information with auditee.
perturbation.changed_text: (g) Not to send Auditee ’s audit information or data and/or any such Confidential Information at any time outside India for the purpose of storage, processing , analysis or handling without the express written consent of the Auditee. (h) The assessor shall use only the best possible secure methodology to avoid confidentiality breach, while handl ing audit related data for the purpose of storage, processing, transit or analysis including sharing of information with auditee.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'auditor' to 'assessor' in subsection (h), the contract now uses two different terms to describe the entity responsible for handling audit-related data. This lack of consistent terminology introduces uncertainty, especially concerning whose methodology is being referred to, while also contradicting subsection (g). This contradicts as well with the initial role definition in the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS A.Auditor is a services organization empanelled by the Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (hereinafter CERT -IN) unde r Department of Electronics & IT , for auditing, including vulnerability assessment and penetration testing of computer systems , networks , computer resources & applications of various agencies or departments of the Government, critical infrastructure orga nizations and those in other sectors of Indian economy vide communication No…………dated……. B.Auditor as an empanelled Information Security Auditing organization has agreed to fully comply the “Guidelines for CERT -In Empanelled Information Security Auditing Organizations , Terms & conditions of empanelment and Policy guidelines for handling audit related data ” while conducting audits.
perturbation.justification: The original text consistently refers to the 'Auditor'. The changed text replaces 'auditor' with 'assessor' in subsection (h). The contradicted text defines the role as 'A. Auditor' and emphasizes that 'B. Auditor' must comply with auditing guidelines. This change introduces a contradiction because the contract now uses two different terms (Auditor and Assessor) to refer to the entity handling audit data, while the contradicted text clearly defines the entity as the 'Auditor', creating ambiguity and potential conflict over who is responsible and whose methodologies apply. This misalignment of terminology creates an in-text contradiction as described in the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Exceptions. The Confidentiality obligations as enumerated in Article 2 of this Agreement shall not apply in following cases : (a) Which is independently developed by Auditor or lawfully received from another source free of restriction and without breach of this Agreement; or (b) After it has become generally available to the public without breach of this Agreement by Auditor ; or
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Exceptions. The Confidentiality obligations as enumerated in Article 2 of this Agreement shall not apply in following cases : (a) Which is independently developed by the Analyst or lawfully received from another source free of restriction and without breach of this Agreement; or (b) After it has become generally available to the public without breach of this Agreement by Auditor ; or
perturbation.explanation: In Section 5, changing 'Auditor' to 'Analyst' in clause (a) creates ambiguity about who is responsible for independently developing information. Because clause (b) still refers to 'Auditor,' it raises concerns about who must avoid breaching the agreement for information to be considered public. This contradiction can affect the agreement of Confidentiality and open discussions for potential unforced errors.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) After it has become generally available to the public without breach of this Agreement by Auditor ; or
perturbation.justification: The change in Section 5(a) from 'Auditor' to 'Analyst' creates a contradiction because it introduces ambiguity about who is responsible for independently developing information that is exempt from confidentiality obligations. The contradicted text, Section 5(b), still refers to 'Auditor,' creating confusion about whose breach of the agreement affects the public availability of the information. This misalignment of terminology, where both 'Auditor' and 'Analyst' are now referenced in the same exception clause, leads to conflicting interpretations and uncertainty, fulfilling the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 18. Non-solicitation. During the term of this Agreement and thereafter for a further period of two (2) years Auditor shall not solicit or attempt to solicit Auditee ’s employees and/or consultants, for the purpose of hiring/contract or to proceed to conduct busi ness similar to Auditee with any employee and/or consultant of the Auditee who has knowledge of the Confidential Information, without the prior written consent of Auditee.
perturbation.changed_text: 18. Non-solicitation. During the term of this Agreement and thereafter for a further period of two (2) years Investigator shall not solicit or attempt to solicit Auditee ’s employees and/or consultants, for the purpose of hiring/contract or to proceed to conduct busi ness similar to Auditee with any employee and/or consultant of the Auditee who has knowledge of the Confidential Information, without the prior written consent of Auditee.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Auditor' to 'Investigator' in the non-solicitation clause creates ambiguity as to who is restricted from soliciting employees. If the 'Auditor' is the party empanelled for audits, introducing 'Investigator' suggests a different role or entity, thereby weakening the enforceability of the non-solicitation clause and contradicting the initially well-defined role, and contradicting the scope of work definition. This creates concerns since 'Auditor' and 'Investigator' are nowhere being related or even having definitions that could relate them to the same party or entity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS A.Auditor is a services organization empanelled by the Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (hereinafter CERT -IN) unde r Department of Electronics & IT , for auditing, including vulnerability assessment and penetration testing of computer systems , networks , computer resources & applications of various agencies or departments of the Government, critical infrastructure orga nizations and those in other sectors of Indian economy vide communication No…………dated……. B.Auditor as an empanelled Information Security Auditing organization has agreed to fully comply the “Guidelines for CERT -In Empanelled Information Security Auditing Organizations , Terms & conditions of empanelment and Policy guidelines for handling audit related data ” while conducting audits.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Auditor' to 'Investigator' in the non-solicitation clause introduces ambiguity and contradicts the definition of 'Auditor' in the 'WHEREAS' clause. The contradicted text defines 'Auditor' as an empanelled services organization by CERT-IN for auditing. By replacing 'Auditor' with 'Investigator,' the non-solicitation clause now applies to an undefined entity, potentially different from the empanelled 'Auditor', which weakens the enforceability of the clause and creates a direct conflict and inconsistency regarding who is bound by the non-solicitation agreement. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.
